concepts.lesson: Rain is extremely important for farmers because crops depend on water to grow. In many villages, farming relies on natural rainfall rather than irrigation. When rains are delayed, farmers become worried because their crops and livelihood are at risk.
concepts.real_example: Velu waits every morning hoping that rain will fall so that his crops can grow properly.
key_points: Rain is essential for crop growth
Farmers depend on seasonal rainfall
Lack of rain can damage crops
Agriculture is closely connected with nature

concepts.lesson: The story shows the hardworking life of farmers. Farmers wake up early, prepare the soil, and work continuously in the fields. Their success depends not only on their hard work but also on natural factors such as rainfall and climate.
concepts.real_example: Velu wakes up early every day and works hard in the fields even though the rain has not arrived.
key_points: Farmers work hard throughout the year
Their work begins early in the morning
Nature strongly influences their success
Farming requires patience and dedication

concepts.lesson: A drought occurs when an area receives very little rainfall for a long time. During droughts, the land becomes dry and crops fail to grow. This causes serious problems for farmers because their fields cannot produce food.
concepts.real_example: Velu’s fields remain dry because the rain does not arrive for many weeks.
key_points: Drought happens due to lack of rainfall
Dry land cannot support crops
Farmers face economic hardship during drought
Food production decreases

concepts.lesson: The story highlights the importance of patience and hope during difficult times. Even when the rains are delayed, farmers continue to wait and hope that nature will eventually bring relief.
concepts.real_example: Velu looks at the sky every day and hopes that the clouds will bring rain soon.
key_points: Patience is necessary during difficult situations
Hope helps people stay positive
Farmers rely on nature’s cycles
Waiting requires emotional strength

concepts.lesson: The story shows how closely human life is connected to nature. Farmers depend on natural elements such as rain, sunlight, and fertile soil. When nature does not cooperate, people must struggle to survive.
concepts.real_example: Velu watches the sky carefully every morning to see if clouds appear.
key_points: Humans depend on natural resources
Weather affects agriculture
Nature influences daily life
Respect for nature is important

concepts.lesson: Waiting for rain creates emotional stress for farmers. They feel anxious and worried about their crops and their future. The story shows how uncertainty can affect a farmer’s thoughts and feelings.
concepts.real_example: Velu feels disappointed when he looks at the sky and sees no clouds.
key_points: Farmers feel anxious during drought
Uncertainty causes emotional stress
Hope and fear exist together
Weather affects both livelihood and emotions

concepts.lesson: The story encourages readers to understand the importance of protecting natural resources. Water conservation and environmental care are necessary to support farming and sustain life.
concepts.real_example: The dry fields in the story remind readers how important rainfall and water resources are.
key_points: Water is a valuable natural resource
Environmental balance is important
Nature must be respected and protected
Human survival depends on natural systems

concepts.lesson: The story follows a narrative structure that begins with the introduction of Velu and his farmland. The conflict arises when the rains fail to arrive and the land becomes dry. The tension builds as the waiting continues, showing the struggle between hope and uncertainty.
concepts.real_example: Velu repeatedly checks the sky every morning, which builds suspense in the story.
key_points: Stories have introduction, conflict, and development
The drought creates the main conflict
Suspense grows as the rain delays
The narrative focuses on emotional and environmental themes
